description_long: This is a vintage tabletop radio receiver with a curved wooden cabinet and a classic mid-century design. The front features a large beige speaker grille, two prominent round control knobs, and a horizontal frequency display with several push buttons below it. The wood casing has a warm, polished finish, and the radio stands on four small angled legs. The back panel is black, perforated for ventilation, and includes various slots and labels, likely for connections and servicing. The sides are smooth and unadorned, highlighting the elegant curvature of the wood. This object would typically be found in a living room, garage, or retro-themed urban setting.
description: A vintage wooden tabletop radio receiver with a curved body, beige speaker grille, and prominent control knobs. The design is classic mid-century with a polished wood finish and a detailed frequency display.
description_view_0: Side view showing the smooth, curved wooden panel and the edge of the speaker grille.
description_view_1: Rear view displaying the black perforated back panel with slots and labels for connections.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, mirroring the smooth, curved wooden panel and the edge of the speaker grille.
description_view_3: Front view highlighting the beige speaker grille, two large control knobs, frequency display, and push buttons.

license_info.creator_display_name: Museum of Engineering and Technology, Krakow
